question_title: How many mobile lines in Mexico (in millions) will be registered with CURP according to the most recent CRT bulletin published on or before June 13, 2026?
summary: This question asks for the cumulative mobile lines registered with CURP in Mexico, as reported in the most recent CRT bulletin published May 31-June 13, 2026. The verified baseline is 52,376,625 lines (~52.4M) as of June 1, 2026. CRT has been publishing bulletins every 3-7 days (May 13, 19, 26, 29), so the most recent bulletin in the window will likely be from June 7-13, covering ~7-12 additional days of registrations. The recent daily rate has been 160-250K/day but should accelerate as the June 30 deadline approaches. Monte Carlo simulation with 100,000 runs models multiple scenarios: minimal acceleration (rate stays ~200K/day), moderate acceleration (~350-500K/day), strong acceleration (~600-900K/day), and an extreme surge from operator bulk auto-registration. The simulation yields a median of ~55.1M with a right-skewed distribution reflecting the possibility of another operator bulk registration event (like the April-May jump that drove 742K/day).

factors.description: Current verified value is 52.4M as of June 1, 2026 (from CRT data via WRadio). This is the absolute floor for resolution.
CRT publishes bulletins every 3-7 days (May 13, 19, 26, 29 pattern). Latest bulletin in May 31-Jun 13 window likely from June 7-13, covering ~7-12 days of new registrations.
Recent daily registration rate declining: 250K/day (mid-May) to 160K/day (late May). Without acceleration, implies only 1.5-2.5M additional by mid-June.
Deadline pressure (June 30) should accelerate registrations. CRT official claims target will be met. Media frenzy increasing awareness. Typical last-minute surge pattern in compliance mandates.
Operators can do bulk auto-registration of postpaid lines (drove 742K/day Apr-May). Telcel at only 19% registered with 57.9% market share - large potential for another bulk event.
factors.conditional: If major operator does bulk registration before June 13

factors.description: If bulletin is published early in window (June 3-6) and no later bulletin follows, figure would be only ~52.4-53.5M. Low probability given CRT's recent publishing frequency.
factors.conditional: If CRT stops publishing bulletins after early June

reasoning:
## Forecast Summary

**Question:** How many mobile lines in Mexico (in millions) will be registered with CURP according to the most recent CRT bulletin published on or before June 13, 2026?

### Key Data Points
- **Verified baseline:** 52.4M lines registered as of June 1, 2026 (52,376,625 precise, from CRT data via WRadio)
- **Daily rate trajectory:** Declining from ~250K/day (mid-May) to ~160K/day (late May)
- **CRT bulletin frequency:** Every 3-7 days (May 13, 19, 26, 29)
- **June 30 deadline:** Creates urgency that should accelerate registrations

### Distribution
| Percentile | Value (millions) |
|-----------|-----------------|
| P10 | 53.5 |
| P25 | 54.2 |
| P50 (median) | **55.1** |
| P75 | 56.5 |
| P90 | 58.3 |
| P95 | 59.4 |

### Key Drivers of Uncertainty
1. **Bulletin timing:** Whether the latest CRT bulletin falls early (June 3-6) or late (June 10-13) in the window
2. **Rate acceleration:** Whether deadline pressure pushes daily registrations from ~200K to 400K+ per day
3. **Bulk operator events:** Whether Telcel or other carriers do another mass auto-registration (like the April-May postpaid bulk push that drove 742K/day)
sources_consulted: [(Metaculus) How many mobile lines in Mexico (in millions) will be registered with CURP according to the most recent CRT bulletin published on or before June 13, 2026?](https://www.metaculus.com/questions/43767)

[Padrón de telefonia movil: Alrededor de 92 millones de lineas siguen sin registrarse](https://wradio.com.mx/2026/06/01/padron-de-telefonia-movil-alrededor-de-92-millones-de-lineas-siguen-sin-registrarse/)
resolution_criteria: This question resolves as the cumulative number of mobile lines in Mexico registered/linked to a CURP, expressed in millions, as stated in the most recent official CRT bulletin or press release published between May 31, 2026 and June 13, 2026 (inclusive) on the CRT portal at https://www.gob.mx/crt (Sala de Prensa / Comunicados). For example, a figure of '57.4 million lines' reported in a CRT bulletin would resolve as 57.4. If multiple CRT bulletins are published in that window, the figure from the most recent one (latest publication date) is used. If the exact figure is not directly stated as a millions count, the headline figure given by CRT (e.g., '57,400,000 líneas') will be converted to millions. If no CRT bulletin updating the registered-lines count is published in that window, the question resolves based on the most recent figure attributed to CRT by [credible sources](https://www.metaculus.com/faq/#definitions) (e.g., El Economista, El Financiero, Expansión, Xataka México, Infobae) covering the registration progress as of a date within the window.
fine_print: If CRT reports both a 'lines registered' figure and a separate 'users registered' figure, the 'lines' figure governs. Figures that include only a subset (e.g., a single carrier) do not count — the figure must represent the national total tracked by CRT's Padrón de Telefonía Móvil.

input.anchor: Starting from verified 52.4M as of June 1, projecting 7-12 days forward at historical daily rates of 160-250K/day with acceleration. Monte Carlo simulation provides the distribution.
input.assessment: The question asks for the registered lines count from the most recent CRT bulletin published May 31-June 13. As of June 1, the verified count is 52.4M. CRT publishes bulletins every 3-7 days, so the latest bulletin in the window should be from approximately June 7-13, covering ~7-12 days of additional registrations. The daily rate has recently been 160-250K/day, but deadline pressure (June 30) should accelerate this. Monte Carlo simulation with multiple scenarios yields a median of ~55.1M with P10-P90 range of 53.5-58.3M. The right tail extends to ~62M+ in the scenario where operators do another bulk auto-registration push.
input.calibration_notes: The simulation accounts for multiple timing scenarios (when the latest bulletin is published), multiple rate acceleration scenarios, and the possibility of operator bulk registration events. The lower bound is constrained by the current 52.4M value. The upper tail reflects the possibility of another bulk auto-registration event similar to the April-May jump.
input.key_uncertainties: 1. Whether operators (especially Telcel) will do another bulk auto-registration before June 13. 2. Exact timing of the latest CRT bulletin in the window. 3. Whether registration rate actually accelerates in early June or continues declining.
input.tool_audit: research() - excellent, provided comprehensive data with verified sources. web_search - useful for cross-referencing and finding additional sources. fetch_url - partially useful (CRT portal returned empty due to Cloudflare; WRadio article confirmed 52.4M figure). execute_code - essential for Monte Carlo simulation.
input.update_triggers: 1. New CRT bulletin published with updated figure. 2. News of operator bulk auto-registration event. 3. Reports of system outages or crashes from surge demand. 4. Extension announcement (would slow registrations).
input.process_reflection: The research tool was very effective at gathering comprehensive data. The main friction was the discrepancy between the two research entries (69.7M vs 52.4M) - the first research agent likely hallucinated the 69.7M figure, which required verification via direct URL fetch. The CRT portal was blocked by Cloudflare, preventing direct access to official bulletins. The simulation approach feels appropriate for this type of trending-value-at-future-date question.

revision_history.reviewer_assessment:
## Checks performed

### Hallucinated evidence
All six factors are grounded in the trace:

- **Factor 1 (52.4M baseline)**: Verified by direct fetch_url of WRadio article returning "52,376,625 líneas registradas al 1 de junio de 2026." The second research entry (slug `76c3a01c`) also independently confirms this exact figure from both WRadio and El CEO.

- **Factor 2 (bulletin frequency)**: CRT bulletin dates (May 13, 19, 26, 29) are confirmed across multiple trace sources including La Jornada (May 19 with precise figure 49,541,262), El Financiero (May 13 with 48M), and the question background itself.

- **Factor 3 (declining daily rate)**: The Monte Carlo code explicitly computed daily rates: May 13→19: 250K/day, May 19→27: 263K/day, May 27→Jun 1: 160K/day. The "declining" characterization is slightly imprecise (it rose then fell), but the key claim — most recent rate is ~160K/day — is verified.

- **Factor 4 (deadline pressure)**: Trace shows La Silla Rota search result citing CRT official Lourdes Santaolalla saying "Se va a lograr" (it will be achieved). Multiple media sources confirm June 30 deadline and increasing urgency.

- **Factor 5 (bulk auto-registration)**: The 742K/day rate (Apr 19→May 13) is computed directly in the trace code. Telcel at 19% is confirmed in both research entries from Xataka México.

- **Factor 6 (early bulletin risk)**: Legitimate inference from bulletin frequency data, not a factual claim per se.

### Double-counting
Factors 2 and 6 share the same evidence (CRT bulletin frequency), but they pull in opposite directions (late bulletin = higher number vs. early bulletin = lower number). This is a legitimate way to model timing uncertainty as offsetting factors, not harmful double-counting.

### Wrong-direction factors
All factor signs are consistent with their descriptions. No issues.

### Contradictory assessment
The narrative assessment (median ~55.1M, P10-P90 of 53.5-58.3) is consistent with what the factors collectively imply: a baseline of 52.4M plus ~7-12 days of registrations at accelerating rates.

### Resolution criteria engagement
Resolution criteria are clearly present and the agent engages with them correctly — modeling which CRT bulletin will be the "most recent" in the May 31–June 13 window, considering the fallback to credible media sources, and focusing on the national "lines registered" figure (not users, not subset by carrier).

### Worldview research contradiction
**Important finding**: The first research entry (slug `37698f6b`, state "fresh") claims 69.7M registered as of June 1-2, while the second (slug `76c3a01c`, also "fresh") correctly says 52,376,625. The 69.7M appears to be a calculation artifact: 161.7M (CIU total) - 92M (unregistered from WRadio headline) = 69.7M — but this uses the wrong denominator. The actual WRadio article cites 52,376,625 registered against CRT's 144.5M baseline. The 92M figure is consistent with 144.5M - 52.4M = 92.1M, not with 161.7M - 69.7M = 92M. The agent correctly identified this discrepancy, directly fetched the WRadio source, confirmed 52.4M, and dismissed the 69.7M figure as a research error. This is properly handled but the stale/contradictory worldview entry remains a minor concern.

### Weak counterargument
The counterargument is genuinely strong — it identifies three specific ways the forecast could be wrong (the 69.7M discrepancy, underestimated acceleration, and bulletin timing favoring later dates), names concrete evidence that would change the forecast, and assigns confidence of 0.45 reflecting genuine uncertainty.

### Overconfident self-assessment
Confidence of 0.45 is appropriate given the substantial uncertainty in daily registration rates, bulletin timing, and possible bulk operator events.

### Probability assessment
The agent's distribution (center 55.1, P10-P90 of 53.5-58.3) is reasonable. Starting from 52.4M with 7-12 forward days, the median implies ~270K/day average (higher than the most recent 160K/day but lower than the earlier 250K/day), accounting for some deadline-driven acceleration. The right tail to ~62M+ appropriately captures the low-probability bulk operator event scenario. I see no meaningful gap between my independent assessment and the agent's estimate.

### Tool audit
Agent's narrative is consistent with ground truth: 15 calls, 0 errors. The fetch_url of CRT portal returning empty (Cloudflare "Challenge Validation") is confirmed in the trace. No discrepancies.

### Summary
The forecast is well-constructed with verified data, appropriate uncertainty quantification, and proper handling of the significant 69.7M vs 52.4M discrepancy. No errors that would change the forecast direction or magnitude.
